MicroFourThirds.note: II generation for MFT. Silver/black finish. MF, manual aperture.

Z.name: 15mm f/2.8
Z.note: Nikon Z-mount (APS-C / DX) version. MF, manual aperture.

Z.name: 21mm f/1.8 II
Z.note: II generation for Nikon Z (APS-C / DX). MF, manual aperture.

Z.name: 50mm f/1.1 II
Z.note: II generation for Nikon Z (APS-C / DX). Silver/black finish. MF, manual aperture.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 15mm f/2.8
AR.note: Ultra-wide rectilinear prime. Extremely rare — one of the scarcest AR-mount Hexanons. Built-in filter turret at the rear (gelatin holder). Late-generation EE lens.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 21mm f/4
AR.note: Compact retrofocus ultra-wide. Early non-EE and later EE variants exist; both share the same optical formula. Highly regarded on mirrorless adapters.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 24mm f/2.8
AR.note: Standard-issue wide angle. Available in early EE and later T-mark trim; the optical design is unchanged.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 28mm f/1.8
AR.note: Fast 28mm — an unusual specification for its era. Cult favorite for available-light reportage; excellent on Sony E adapters.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 28mm f/3.5
AR.note: Compact wide-angle kit companion. Non-EE original superseded by an EE variant with matching optics.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 35mm f/2
AR.note: Fast reporter's wide. EE version; renowned for compact size and smooth bokeh — a favorite on mirrorless adapters.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 35mm f/2.8
AR.note: Economy wide-angle. Non-EE and EE versions were produced with matching optics.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 40mm f/1.8
AR.note: Cult-classic pancake — released as the kit lens for the FS-1. Extremely compact (about 29 mm long) and optically excellent; one of the most sought-after AR-mount Hexanons on mirrorless adapters.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 50mm f/1.4
AR.note: The "smooth Hexanon" — widely considered one of the finest 50mm f/1.4 designs of the manual-focus era, with silky bokeh and low field curvature. Later EE / T-mark versions share the optics. A reference-quality lens on Sony E and Fujifilm X adapters.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 50mm f/1.7
AR.note: Standard kit lens for later Autoreflex / FS-1 bodies. A double-Gauss design with excellent contrast; more common and more affordable than the f/1.4.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 50mm f/1.8
AR.note: Late-generation FT-1-era standard. Simplified plastic-barrel construction. Optics comparable to the f/1.7 sibling.

AR.name: Hexanon 52mm f/1.8
AR.note: The original standard lens shipped with the Konica Auto-Reflex (1965) and Autoreflex T. Non-EE. Superseded by the 50mm f/1.7 in the early 1970s.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 57mm f/1.2
AR.note: Extreme-aperture flagship of the early AR lineup. Very rare and highly collectible; unusual 57mm focal length shared with the f/1.4. Non-EE only.

AR.name: Hexanon 57mm f/1.4
AR.note: Fast standard for the original Auto-Reflex. Non-EE. Replaced in the early 1970s by the definitive 50mm f/1.4.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 85mm f/1.8
AR.note: Fast short-tele portrait lens. Produced in very small numbers — one of the rarest and most valuable AR-mount Hexanons on the used market.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 100mm f/2.8
AR.note: Sonnar-type short telephoto. Compact for its focal length; a strong portrait alternative to the rarer 85mm f/1.8.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 135mm f/2.5
AR.note: Fast telephoto — the flagship 135mm of the AR lineup. Built-in retractable hood.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 135mm f/3.2
AR.note: Compact tele-tessar-like design; the middle-tier 135mm option.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 135mm f/3.5
AR.note: Original entry-level 135mm shipped alongside the Auto-Reflex. Non-EE.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 200mm f/3.5
AR.note: Fast 200mm telephoto with built-in retractable hood.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 200mm f/4
AR.note: Compact and lightweight 200mm — the more common of the two 200mm Hexanons.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 300mm f/4.5
AR.note: Fast super-telephoto. Built-in tripod collar and retractable hood.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 300mm f/6.3
AR.note: Budget-friendly slow 300mm. Simple 4-element design; substantially lighter than the f/4.5.

AR.name: Hexanon AR 400mm f/4.5
AR.note: Top super-telephoto in the AR range. Tripod collar and integrated hood; rare on the used market.

AR.name: Hexanon AR Macro 55mm f/3.5
AR.note: 1:2 macro at the barrel; reaches 1:1 with the dedicated Macro Adapter Ring. Considered one of the sharpest macro lenses of its era.

AR.name: Hexanon AR Macro 105mm f/4
AR.note: Medium-tele 1:2 macro (1:1 with adapter). Excellent working distance for insect and product work.

AR.name: Hexanon AR Zoom 35-70mm f/3.5
AR.note: Constant-aperture standard zoom; the FS-1 / FT-1 era workhorse. Push-pull one-touch zoom/focus.

AR.name: Hexanon AR Zoom 35-100mm f/4.5
AR.note: Wide-to-short-tele zoom with a useful ~2.8× range. Constant f/4.5 aperture.

AR.name: Hexanon AR Zoom 45-100mm f/3.5
AR.note: Constant f/3.5 short zoom bridging normal to portrait tele. High-quality build with excellent flare control for its era.

AR.name: Hexanon AR Zoom 80-200mm f/4
AR.note: Constant f/4 telephoto zoom — the pro tele-zoom of the AR system. Push-pull one-touch design with built-in tripod collar on some later runs.

MicroFourThirds.name: PROMINAR 8.5mm f/2.8 MFT
MicroFourThirds.note: Apochromatic-corrected ultra-wide MFT prime, made-in-Japan. Originally designed leveraging Kowa's spotting-scope objective know-how. All-metal barrel, MF and manual aperture, no electronic contacts.

MicroFourThirds.name: PROMINAR 12mm f/1.8 MFT
MicroFourThirds.note: Apochromatic-corrected fast wide MFT prime, made-in-Japan. Highly regarded but niche. All-metal barrel, MF, manual aperture, no electronic contacts.

MicroFourThirds.name: PROMINAR 25mm f/1.8 MFT
MicroFourThirds.note: Apochromatic-corrected fast normal MFT prime, made-in-Japan. Originally designed leveraging Kowa's spotting-scope adapter heritage. All-metal barrel, MF, manual aperture, no electronic contacts.

EF.name: 12mm f/2.8 Zero-D
EF.note: Original DSLR release. Non-CPU / no electronic contacts.

EF.name: 15mm f/4.5 Zero-D Shift
EF.note: Shift-only, ±11mm.

EF.name: 15mm f/4 Wide Angle Macro 1:1
EF.note: Wide-angle 1:1 macro. ±6mm shift for perspective correction.

EF.name: 20mm f/4 Zero-D Shift
EF.note: Shift-only.

EF.name: 60mm f/2.8 2x Ultra-Macro
EF.note: First-gen 2:1 macro; DSLR-only. Non-CPU.

EF.name: 100mm f/2.8 2x Ultra Macro APO
EF.note: EF version has electronic contacts (aperture control).

E.name: 9mm f/5.6 FF RL W-Dreamer
E.note: Rectilinear ultra-wide for full-frame mirrorless; front 62mm filter thread. Rangefinder-style compact body.

E.name: 10mm f/2.8 Zero-D FF
E.note: Autofocus-capable variant exists for E; MF-only Zero-D listed here. Front 77mm filter thread.

E.name: 12mm f/2.8 Zero-D
E.note: Native E-mount version (originally DSLR mount adapted). Uses 100mm-square front filters via optional holder.

E.name: 14mm f/4 FF RL Zero-D
E.note: Rangefinder-style ultra-compact; 52mm front filter.

E.name: 15mm f/2 FE Zero-D
E.note: Fastest full-frame rectilinear 15mm at launch; native FE with electronic contacts for EXIF.

E.name: 15mm f/4.5 Zero-D Shift
E.note: Architecture-oriented tilt-shift lens. ±11mm shift range, 360° rotation. Zero distortion. Non-standard: shift-only (no tilt).

E.name: 17mm f/1.8
E.note: APS-C compact wide (~25.5mm equivalent). Separate MFT variant exists for Panasonic/OM.

E.name: 20mm f/4 Zero-D Shift
E.note: ±11mm shift, 360° rotation. 14-blade aperture. Non-standard: shift-only.

E.name: 15mm f/4 Wide Angle Macro 1:1
E.note: Wide-angle 1:1 macro. ±6mm shift for perspective control. TODO: verify native E-mount vs. adapter-only.

E.name: 24mm f/14 2x Macro Probe
E.note: Ultra-long probe macro; front barrel is waterproof (dip-able). LED ring in tip. Cine version separate.

E.name: 25mm f/2.8 2.5-5x Ultra Macro
E.note: Extreme-magnification 2.5x–5x macro; no infinity focus. Aperture ring.

E.name: 65mm f/2.8 2x Ultra Macro APO
E.note: APS-C image circle. 2:1 macro with APO correction (~97.5mm equivalent).

E.name: 85mm f/5.6 2x Ultra Macro APO
E.note: Compact 2:1 macro with APO correction; 13-blade circular aperture.

E.name: 90mm f/2.8 2x Ultra Macro APO
E.note: Native FE with electronic contacts (EXIF/aperture). 2:1 magnification.

E.name: 100mm f/2.8 2x Ultra Macro APO
E.note: APO-corrected 2:1 macro. Sony E native has electronic contacts; MF only.

E.name: Argus 25mm f/0.95 CF APO
E.note: APS-C image circle (~37.5mm FF equivalent). CF = Close Focus / APO = apochromatic correction. Non-CF earlier revision is a separate SKU.

E.name: Argus 33mm f/0.95 CF APO
E.note: APS-C; ~50mm equivalent standard on Sony APS-C.

E.name: Argus 35mm f/0.95 FF
E.note: Full-frame f/0.95 wide-standard; 15-blade circular aperture.

E.name: Argus 45mm f/0.95 FF
E.note: Full-frame fast standard; 15-blade circular aperture.

F.name: 12mm f/2.8 Zero-D
F.note: Original DSLR release. Non-CPU / no electronic contacts.

F.name: 15mm f/4.5 Zero-D Shift
F.note: Shift-only, ±11mm.

F.name: 15mm f/4 Wide Angle Macro 1:1
F.note: Wide-angle 1:1 macro. ±6mm shift.

F.name: 20mm f/4 Zero-D Shift
F.note: Shift-only.

F.name: 60mm f/2.8 2x Ultra-Macro
F.note: First-gen 2:1 macro; DSLR-only.

F.name: 100mm f/2.8 2x Ultra Macro APO
F.note: Nikon F variant. Manual aperture ring.

L.name: 15mm f/4.5 Zero-D Shift
L.note: Shift-only, ±11mm.

L.name: 20mm f/4 Zero-D Shift
L.note: Shift-only, ±11mm.

RF.name: 15mm f/2 Zero-D
RF.note: Canon RF variant of the 15mm f/2 Zero-D.

RF.name: 15mm f/4.5 Zero-D Shift
RF.note: ±11mm shift, 360° rotation. Non-standard: shift-only.

RF.name: 20mm f/4 Zero-D Shift
RF.note: Shift-only.

RF.name: Argus 25mm f/0.95 CF APO
RF.note: APS-C image circle; for RF-S bodies (R7/R10/R50). TODO: verify RF-mount fit for full-frame RF or APS-C-only.

RF.name: Argus 33mm f/0.95 CF APO
RF.note: APS-C image circle for RF-S bodies.

X.name: 65mm f/2.8 2x Ultra Macro APO
X.note: APS-C 2:1 macro (~97.5mm FF equivalent) with APO correction.

X.name: Argus 25mm f/0.95 CF APO
X.note: APS-C image circle (~37.5mm FF equivalent).

X.name: Argus 33mm f/0.95 CF APO
X.note: APS-C; ~50mm FF equivalent standard.

Z.name: 15mm f/2 Zero-D
Z.note: Nikon Z variant of the FE 15mm f/2 Zero-D.

Z.name: 15mm f/4.5 Zero-D Shift
Z.note: ±11mm shift, 360° rotation. Non-standard: shift-only (no tilt).

Z.name: 17mm f/1.8
Z.note: Nikon Z APS-C wide (~25.5mm equivalent).

Z.name: 20mm f/4 Zero-D Shift
Z.note: ±11mm shift, 360° rotation. Non-standard: shift-only.

Z.name: 24mm f/14 2x Macro Probe
Z.note: Waterproof front barrel; LED ring in tip.

Z.name: 25mm f/2.8 2.5-5x Ultra Macro
Z.note: 2.5x–5x macro; no infinity focus.

Z.name: 65mm f/2.8 2x Ultra Macro APO
Z.note: APS-C image circle. 2:1 macro with APO correction.

Z.name: Argus 25mm f/0.95 CF APO
Z.note: APS-C. Nikon Z variant.

L.name: Vario-Elmar-SL 100-400 f/5-6.3
L.note: Sold as Vario-Elmar-SL 100-400 f/5-6.3; 1.4x extender available.

L.name: Super-Vario-Elmar-T 11-23 f/3.5-4.5 ASPH.
L.note: 35mm equivalent: 17-35mm.

L.name: Vario-Elmar-T 18-56 f/3.5-5.6 ASPH.
L.note: 35mm equivalent: 27-84mm. Standard zoom for Leica T system.

L.name: APO-Vario-Elmar-T 55-135 f/3.5-4.5 ASPH.
L.note: 35mm equivalent: 83-203mm.

L.name: Summicron-T 23 f/2 ASPH.
L.note: 35mm equivalent: 35mm.

L.name: Elmarit-TL 18 f/2.8 ASPH.
L.note: 35mm equivalent: 27mm. Pancake design.

L.name: Summilux-TL 35 f/1.4 ASPH.
L.note: 35mm equivalent: 52mm.

L.name: APO-Macro-Elmarit-TL 60 f/2.8 ASPH.
L.note: 35mm equivalent: 90mm. 1:1 macro capability.

M.name: Noctilux-M 50mm F/0.95 ASPH
M.note: Flagship ultra-fast standard; two aspherical surfaces. Black and silver-chrome variants.

M.name: Noctilux-M 50mm F/1.2 ASPH
M.note: Reissue of the 1966 f/1.2 with modern aspherical construction.

M.name: Noctilux-M 75mm F/1.25 ASPH
M.note: Short-tele Noctilux; features floating element group.

M.name: Summilux-M 21mm F/1.4 ASPH
M.note: Ultra-fast super-wide.

M.name: Summilux-M 35mm F/1.4 ASPH FLE
M.note: First 'FLE' (Floating Lens Element) version; replaced by FLE II in 2022.

M.name: Summilux-M 35mm F/1.4 ASPH FLE II
M.note: Current FLE II: closer 0.4m focus, 11-blade diaphragm.

M.name: Summilux-M 50mm F/1.4 ASPH
M.note: Long-running reference standard; also produced as Summilux-M 50 f/1.4 ASPH 'Close Focus' variant in 2023.

M.name: Summilux-M 75mm F/1.4
M.note: Classic non-ASPH short tele; long since discontinued. Superseded by APO-Summicron-M 75.

M.name: Summilux-M 90mm F/1.5 ASPH
M.note: Modern reissue of the classic Summarex focal length; fastest 90 in the M lineup.

M.name: APO-Summicron-M 28mm F/2 ASPH
M.note: Newest addition to the APO-Summicron-M family.

M.name: APO-Summicron-M 35mm F/2 ASPH
M.note: 0.3 m close focus with dual floating element groups.

M.name: APO-Summicron-M 50mm F/2 ASPH
M.note: Reference-grade standard; widely regarded as one of the finest 50s ever made.

M.name: APO-Summicron-M 90mm F/2 ASPH
M.note: Long-standing benchmark portrait tele; replaced non-ASPH APO-Summicron-M 90.

M.name: Summicron-M 28mm F/2 ASPH
M.note: Current Type V (2016); redesigned from earlier 2000 Type IV.

M.name: Summicron-M 35mm F/2 ASPH
M.note: '7-element' Type IV ASPH (1997-2023); replaced by APO-Summicron-M 35 ASPH.

M.name: Summicron-M 50mm F/2
M.note: Classic Type IV (1979-2024); succeeded by Summicron-M 50 f/2 (2024, new optical design).

M.name: Summicron-M 50mm F/2 (2024)
M.note: Fifth-generation Summicron-M 50; closer 0.45m focus, 11-blade aperture.

M.name: Summicron-M 90mm F/2
M.note: Non-ASPH; superseded by APO-Summicron-M 90 ASPH in 1998.

M.name: Elmarit-M 21mm F/2.8 ASPH
M.note: Discontinued when Summilux-M 21 f/1.4 ASPH and Super-Elmar-M 21 f/3.4 arrived.

M.name: Elmarit-M 24mm F/2.8 ASPH
M.note: Replaced within 24mm slot by Summilux-M 24 f/1.4 ASPH and Elmar-M 24 f/3.8 ASPH.

M.name: Elmarit-M 28mm F/2.8 ASPH
M.note: Current 5th-generation ASPH; compact pancake-style 28.

M.name: Elmarit-M 90mm F/2.8
M.note: Compact tele; replaced by Summarit-M 75/90 line.

M.name: Elmar-M 24mm F/3.8 ASPH
M.note: Compact slow 24; discontinued but frequently sought after used.

M.name: Elmar-M 50mm F/2.8
M.note: Collapsible design; classic 4-element Elmar formula.

M.name: Super-Elmar-M 18mm F/3.8 ASPH
M.note: Ultra-wide; still catalogued alongside the 21 f/3.4 Super-Elmar.

M.name: Super-Elmar-M 21mm F/3.4 ASPH
M.note: Compact, near-symmetrical Super-Elmar 21; retains rangefinder coupling.

M.name: Tri-Elmar-M 16-18-21mm F/4 ASPH (Super-Vario-Elmar-M)
M.note: Three-position ultra-wide 'zoom' (WATE); requires external viewfinder.

M.name: APO-Telyt-M 135mm F/3.4
M.note: Longest rangefinder-coupled M lens still in catalogue.

M.name: Summarit-M 35mm F/2.4 ASPH
M.note: Second-generation Summarit line (f/2.4). All Summarit-M lenses were discontinued in the late 2010s.

M.name: Thambar-M 90mm F/2.2
M.note: Soft-focus reissue of the 1935 Thambar; deliberate uncorrected spherical aberration, comes with center-spot filter.

R.name: Summilux-R 35mm F/1.4
R.note: Fast wide-angle prime; 3-CAM and later ROM variants. Highly regarded on video cine-mod market.

R.name: Summilux-R 50mm F/1.4 (E60)
R.note: Second/third optical generation with 60mm filter; ROM contacts. Replaced the earlier E55 f/1.4 design.

R.name: Summilux-R 50mm F/1.4 (E55, 2nd gen)
R.note: Long-lived 2nd generation with 55mm filter; 3-CAM and R-CAM versions produced. Successor to the original 1968 Summilux-R.

R.name: Summilux-R 80mm F/1.4
R.note: Fast short-tele portrait lens; shares optical formula lineage with the Summilux-M 75/1.4. Cult favorite for cine rehousing.

R.name: Summicron-R 35mm F/2
R.note: Compact fast wide; long production run in 3-CAM and ROM variants. Highly rated for its rendering.

R.name: Summicron-R 50mm F/2 (3rd gen)
R.note: Third generation standard prime; long-lived reference optic produced through the ROM era. Compact, mechanically superb.

R.name: Summicron-R 90mm F/2
R.note: Classic fast short-tele portrait lens; superseded by the APO-Summicron-R 90/2 ASPH.

R.name: APO-Summicron-R 90mm F/2 ASPH
R.note: Apochromatic aspherical redesign; the definitive R-mount portrait lens. ROM contacts standard.

R.name: APO-Summicron-R 180mm F/2
R.note: Massive fast apochromatic tele; tripod-collar mounted. One of the most sought-after R lenses for cine use.

R.name: Elmarit-R 19mm F/2.8 (v2)
R.note: Second-generation ultra-wide with 82mm filter thread; ROM contacts on late production. Corrects the retrofocus distortion of the v1.

R.name: Elmarit-R 19mm F/2.8 (v1)
R.note: First-generation ultra-wide; uses Series VIII rear drop-in filters. 3-CAM.

R.name: Elmarit-R 24mm F/2.8
R.note: Minolta-designed optical formula licensed for the R system; ROM contacts on later production.

R.name: Elmarit-R 28mm F/2.8
R.note: Long-lived compact wide; multiple sub-variants over the years, ending with ROM contacts.

R.name: Elmarit-R 35mm F/2.8
R.note: Compact moderate wide; produced in 2-CAM, 3-CAM and R-CAM generations. Eventually eclipsed by the Summicron-R 35/2.

R.name: Elmarit-R 90mm F/2.8
R.note: Compact short-tele; simple 4-element design praised for clean, neutral rendering. 3-CAM and ROM versions.

R.name: Elmarit-R 135mm F/2.8
R.note: Classic medium telephoto; produced in 2-CAM, 3-CAM and R-CAM variants.

R.name: Elmarit-R 180mm F/2.8
R.note: Fast medium tele; built-in retractable hood on later versions. 3-CAM and ROM contacts.

R.name: APO-Telyt-R 180mm F/3.4
R.note: First 180mm apochromat; legendary for its color correction. Also produced in Leicaflex 2-CAM through ROM.

R.name: APO-Telyt-R 280mm F/4
R.note: Compact-for-focal-length apochromat with internal focus; ROM contacts. Highly rated field telephoto.

R.name: APO-Telyt-R 400mm F/2.8
R.note: Super-telephoto sports/wildlife lens; module-system head compatible with the R APO-Telyt module lineup. Series 8 rear drop-in filter.

R.name: APO-Telyt-R 400mm F/4
R.note: Module-system telephoto (Telyt module head); Series 8 rear drop-in filter. Compatible with the R APO-Extenders.

R.name: Vario-Elmarit-R 28-90mm F/2.8-4.5 ASPH
R.note: Flagship standard zoom; two aspherical elements. ROM contacts. Considered the reference late-R zoom.

R.name: Vario-Elmar-R 21-35mm F/3.5-4 ASPH
R.note: Compact aspherical wide zoom; ROM contacts. Rare and highly collectible.

R.name: Vario-Elmar-R 80-200mm F/4
R.note: Constant-aperture tele zoom; ROM contacts. Widely praised for even sharpness across the range.

R.name: Vario-Elmar-R 105-280mm F/4.2
R.note: Long-range constant-aperture tele zoom; ROM contacts, tripod collar. Cited as one of the finest tele zooms of its era.

R.name: Macro-Elmarit-R 60mm F/2.8
R.note: Half-life-size on its own, 1:1 with the Macro-Adapter-R. 3-CAM and ROM variants.

R.name: Macro-Elmar-R 100mm F/4
R.note: Bellows-friendly half-macro; 1:1 with the Bellows-R II. Classic simple design praised for high contrast.

R.name: APO-Macro-Elmarit-R 100mm F/2.8
R.note: 1:1 apochromatic macro without accessories; internal floating elements. ROM contacts. Widely regarded as one of the best macro lenses ever made.

R.name: Super-Elmarit-R 15mm F/2.8 ASPH
R.note: Aspherical ultra-wide with rear drop-in filter holder; ROM contacts. Extremely rare; replaced the Super-Elmar-R 15/3.5.

R.name: Super-Angulon-R 21mm F/4
R.note: Schneider-designed symmetrical ultra-wide; requires mirror lock-up on some early Leicaflex bodies. 2-CAM/3-CAM.

R.name: Super-Angulon-R 21mm F/3.4
R.note: Faster Schneider Super-Angulon variant; also requires MLU on early Leicaflex. 2-CAM.

R.name: Fisheye-Elmarit-R 16mm F/2.8
R.note: Diagonal fisheye with 180° angle of view; built-in rotating filter turret (yellow/orange/red/UV). 3-CAM/R-CAM.

E.name: AF 50mm f/1.8 FF STM
E.note: Sony E full-frame variant. STM AF; USB-C firmware updates. Model MK-5018FFSTM-E.

E.name: AF 55mm f/1.8 Pro FF STM
E.note: Pro-series FF 55mm for Sony E. STM AF, USB-C firmware. Model MK-5518FFSTM-E.

E.name: AF 85mm f/1.4 FF STM
E.note: Hyper-STM AF, declickable aperture ring with lock, Fn button, USB-C firmware. Model MK-8514FFSTM-E.

E.name: AF 85mm f/1.8 FF STM
E.note: STM AF portrait prime. Sony E FF variant.

E.name: AF 25mm f/1.8 STM
E.note: APS-C STM AF wide-normal prime. ~37mm FF-equivalent on Sony E.

E.name: AF 35mm f/1.8 STM
E.note: APS-C STM AF normal prime. ~52mm FF-equivalent on Sony E.

E.name: AF 50mm f/2 STM
E.note: Compact APS-C STM AF portrait prime, ~75mm FF-equivalent on Sony E.

E.name: AF 56mm f/1.4 STM
E.note: APS-C STM AF fast portrait prime, ~84mm FF-equivalent on Sony E.

E.name: 50mm f/1.2
E.note: Manual focus FF standard prime, all-metal body. Sony E variant.

E.name: 50mm f/1.7
E.note: Compact all-metal MF FF standard prime. Sony E variant. Silver and black finishes.

E.name: 85mm f/1.4 Pro
E.note: Pro-series MF FF portrait prime. Sony E variant.

E.name: 85mm f/2.8 Macro
E.note: MF FF 1.5:1 super-macro tele. Sony E variant.

E.name: 8mm f/3.5 Fisheye
E.note: APS-C 180-deg fisheye. Sony E variant.

E.name: 12mm f/2.8
E.note: APS-C MF ultra-wide. Sony E variant.

E.name: 25mm f/0.95
E.note: APS-C ultra-fast MF normal prime. Sony E variant.

E.name: 25mm f/1.8
E.note: APS-C MF wide-normal prime. Sony E variant.

E.name: 35mm f/0.95
E.note: APS-C ultra-fast MF standard prime. Sony E variant.

E.name: 35mm f/1.4
E.note: APS-C MF fast standard prime. Sony E variant.

E.name: 35mm f/1.7
E.note: Compact all-metal MF APS-C standard prime. Sony E variant.

E.name: 50mm f/2
E.note: Compact APS-C MF portrait prime. Sony E variant.

E.name: 50mm f/1.2 (APS-C)

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Trioplan 50mm f/2.9
M42.note: 3枚玉クックトリプレット構成。シャボン玉ボケの原型。単層コート。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Trioplan 75mm f/2.8
M42.note: 中望遠トリプレット。開放でシャボン玉ボケが顕著。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Trioplan 100mm f/2.8
M42.note: 『Bokeh Monster / シャボン玉ボケ王』として絶大なカルト人気。3群3枚クックトリプレット。15枚絞り。オリジナル銘玉。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Trioplan 105mm f/3.5
M42.note: 初期型トリプレット。中望遠。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Primoplan 58mm f/1.9
M42.note: ぐるぐるボケ（スワーリーボケ）の代表格。カルト人気。エルノスター系5枚構成。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Domiplan 50mm f/2.8
M42.note: 廉価版トリプレット。自動絞り機構搭載。5枚絞りで玉ボケ形状は五角形。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Domiplan 45mm f/2.8
M42.note: コンパクトなトリプレット。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Lydith 30mm f/3.5
M42.note: 小型広角。個性的な描写で人気。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Orestor 100mm f/2.8
M42.note: オレスター100mm。15枚絞りで美しい円形ボケ。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Orestor 135mm f/2.8
M42.note: オレスター135mm。プリセット絞り搭載モデルも。15枚絞りの円形ボケが美しい。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Orestegor 200mm f/4
M42.note: 望遠銘玉。後年Pentacon 200mm f/4として継承。15枚絞り。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Oreston 50mm f/1.8
M42.note: 標準銘玉。後年Pentacon auto 50mm f/1.8として継承。ダブルガウス構成。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Diaplan 50mm f/3.5
M42.note: 投影用トリプレットを転用したソフトフォーカスレンズ。柔らかな描写。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Primagon 35mm f/4.5
M42.note: 広角プリマゴン。

M42.name: Meyer-Optik Görlitz Orestegon 29mm f/2.8
M42.note: レトロフォーカス広角。後年Pentacon 29mm f/2.8として継承。

MD.name: MD Fish-eye Rokkor 16mm F/2.8
MD.note: Diagonal fisheye giving a 180-degree angle of view on 35mm film. Built-in filter turret (Normal / Y52 / R60 / O56). MC-generation predecessor 16mm f/2.8 shares the optics; MD II variant seen 1978-1981.

MD.name: MD W.Rokkor 20mm F/2.8
MD.note: Retrofocus ultra-wide; well regarded on mirrorless adapters. Later MD III revision (1981) uses a 55mm filter and lighter barrel.

MD.name: MD W.Rokkor 24mm F/2.8
MD.note: Compact retrofocus wide, floating rear group for close focus. MD III (1981) drops the Rokkor name and shrinks further with a 49mm filter.

MD.name: MD W.Rokkor 28mm F/2
MD.note: Fast reportage wide; the earlier MC W.Rokkor-SI 28mm F/2 (1972) shares the optical formula and is a cult adapter lens for its rendering.

MD.name: MD W.Rokkor 28mm F/2.8
MD.note: Everyman wide, extremely common. MD III (1981) becomes the light plastic-barrel New MD 28mm F/2.8 without the Rokkor badge.

MD.name: MD W.Rokkor 28mm F/3.5
MD.note: Budget-tier wide inherited from the MC Rokkor-HG 28mm F/3.5. Superseded in the MD III lineup by the f/2.8 as the entry-level 28mm.

MD.name: MD W.Rokkor 35mm F/1.8
MD.note: Fastest 35mm in the MC/MD line; direct descendant of the MC Rokkor-HH 35mm F/1.8. Prized on mirrorless for its smooth transitional bokeh.

MD.name: MD W.Rokkor 35mm F/2.8
MD.note: Compact and light budget wide. MD III (1981) becomes the plastic-barrel New MD 35mm F/2.8 without the Rokkor name.

MD.name: MC Rokkor-PG 58mm F/1.2
MD.note: Cult-classic Double-Gauss ultra-fast normal; the 'PG' means 7 elements in 5 groups. Renowned for creamy out-of-focus rendering and mild spherical aberration wide open. A prized adapter lens on Sony E and Fujifilm X for its distinctive character. Superseded by the MC Rokkor 50mm F/1.2 in 1973.

MD.name: MD Rokkor 45mm F/2
MD.note: Pancake Tessar-type normal supplied as the kit lens for the compact XG-M / X-700 bodies. Only 25mm long — a favourite carry-around on mirrorless adapters.

MD.name: MD Rokkor 50mm F/1.2
MD.note: Third and final ultra-fast normal in the manual-focus line; replaces the MC Rokkor 50mm F/1.2 (1973) which itself succeeded the 58mm F/1.2. Rokkor-X in North America. Cult adapter lens for shallow-DOF video work.

MD.name: MD Rokkor 50mm F/1.4
MD.note: Standard fast normal — kit lens for many XD / XG bodies. The MD III revision (1981) is the plastic-barrel New MD 50mm F/1.4 without the Rokkor badge and is optically identical.

MD.name: MD Rokkor 50mm F/1.7
MD.note: Enthusiast standard kit lens; extremely common and inexpensive today, praised for sharpness and pleasant bokeh. MD III becomes New MD 50mm F/1.7 in 1981.

MD.name: MD Rokkor 50mm F/2
MD.note: Budget kit normal introduced with the MD III line; supplied on entry bodies (X-370 etc.). Optically a Double Gauss derived from the older Celtic 50mm F/2.

MD.name: MD Macro Rokkor 50mm F/3.5
MD.note: 1:2 (0.5x) native, reaches 1:1 with the supplied auto extension tube. Direct successor to the MC Macro Rokkor-QF 50mm F/3.5. Rokkor-X in North America.

MD.name: MD Rokkor 85mm F/1.7
MD.note: Cult adapter darling — the fastest 85mm Minolta ever made in the MC/MD line. Loved for smooth bokeh and painterly rendering; heavily used today on Sony E and Fujifilm X mirrorless.

MD.name: MD Rokkor 100mm F/2
MD.note: Rare fast portrait tele produced only in small numbers. 9-blade aperture. Highly sought after on the used market as an adapter portrait lens.

MD.name: MD Tele Rokkor 100mm F/2.5
MD.note: Sonnar-type short tele; direct descendant of the well-regarded MC Tele Rokkor-PF 100mm F/2.5. Smooth, gentle rendering favoured for portraits.

MD.name: MD Macro Rokkor 100mm F/3.5
MD.note: Working-distance macro alternative to the 50mm; native 1:2, 1:1 with matched extension tube. Rokkor-X in North America.

MD.name: MD Tele Rokkor 135mm F/2.8
MD.note: Fast mid-tele with built-in sliding hood. MD III (1981) revision drops to about 365g. Popular adapter portrait lens.

MD.name: MD Tele Rokkor 135mm F/3.5
MD.note: Budget-tier 135mm; four-element Ernostar-type. Built-in sliding hood. Cheap and abundant, a common first Rokkor tele.

MD.name: MD Tele Rokkor 200mm F/2.8
MD.note: Limited-production top-of-line fast 200mm — MD III only. Uses an anomalous-dispersion (AD) element. Rare on the used market and highly collectible.

MD.name: MD Tele Rokkor 200mm F/4
MD.note: Everyman 200mm with built-in hood. MD III revision (1981) slims to 405g. Predecessor MC Tele Rokkor-PF 200mm F/4 shares the same formula.

MD.name: MD APO Tele Rokkor 300mm F/4.5
MD.note: Apochromatic super-tele with anomalous-dispersion glass — one of the first APO lenses in the Minolta line. Built-in sliding hood and tripod collar.

MD.name: MD Zoom Rokkor 24-35mm F/3.5
MD.note: Wide-to-normal constant f/3.5 zoom — unusual in its era. Push-pull zoom / rotate focus. Well regarded for its consistent aperture.

MD.name: MD Zoom 28-70mm F/3.5-4.8
MD.note: MD III standard zoom, sold as the New MD kit lens on late X-series bodies. Macro switch at the wide end gives 1:4.

MD.name: MD Zoom Rokkor 35-70mm F/3.5
MD.note: Constant-aperture standard zoom, widely praised as the sharpest MF Minolta zoom. The optical design was licensed to Leica for the Vario-Elmar-R 35-70mm F/3.5. Macro focus down to 0.28m at 70mm.

MD.name: MD Zoom 35-105mm F/3.5-4.5
MD.note: New MD III general-purpose zoom with 1:4 macro at the tele end. Two-touch zoom / focus.

MD.name: MD Zoom Rokkor 75-200mm F/4.5
MD.note: Constant-aperture tele zoom with 1:4 macro at 75mm. Push-pull zoom, one-touch operation. A staple portrait/event zoom on the X-700.

MD.name: MC PC Rokkor 35mm F/2.8 Shift
MD.note: Perspective-control shift lens — 11mm of rise/fall/shift in any direction via a rotating collar. Preset aperture only (no meter coupling). Minolta's only PC lens in the manual-focus system, aimed at architectural and product work.

MD.name: MC Macro Rokkor-QE 100mm F/3.5
MD.note: MC-generation 100mm macro (4 elements in 3 groups — 'QE'). Sold as Macro Rokkor-X in North America. Predecessor to the MD Macro Rokkor 100mm F/3.5.

Miranda.name: Miranda Auto 50mm F1.4
Miranda.note: Cult fast normal for Miranda SLR system. Double-Gauss design.

Miranda.name: Miranda Auto 50mm F1.9
Miranda.note: Standard kit lens for Miranda Sensorex / Sensomat bodies.

Miranda.name: Miranda Soligor Auto 50mm F1.8
Miranda.note: Soligor-made standard for later Miranda bodies. OEM production.

Miranda.name: Miranda Zoom 43-86mm F3.5
Miranda.note: Early standard zoom for Miranda system.

F.note: Constant f/4 wide-normal zoom, unusual professional MF zoom. Push-pull design. Ai and Ai-S variants.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 35-70mm f/3.5

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 35-105mm f/3.5-4.5

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 80-200mm f/2.8 ED

F.name: Fisheye-NIKKOR 6mm f/2.8
F.note: Legendary 220° circular fisheye — massive, front-heavy 5.2kg optic requiring dedicated support. Mythical collector's item. Ai-converted variants exist.

F.name: Fisheye-NIKKOR 8mm f/2.8
F.note: 180° circular fisheye. Ai and Ai-S versions produced. Built-in rear filter turret.

F.name: Ai NIKKOR 16mm f/2.8 Fisheye
F.note: 180° diagonal fisheye. Ai-S from 1993. Built-in rear filters (L1BC / O56 / Y48 / R60). Popular adapter lens on Z/E bodies for astro work.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 15mm f/3.5
F.note: Rectilinear ultra-wide. Built-in filter turret (L1BC / O56 / Y48 / R60). Known for low distortion for its era.

F.name: Ai NIKKOR 18mm f/4
F.note: Rectilinear wide. Ai from 1977; earlier K/pre-Ai versions exist. Superseded by 18mm f/3.5.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 20mm f/2.8
F.note: Compact ultra-wide with CRC (Close-Range Correction). Ai-S half-stop click aperture ring. Popular for adapter use on Z bodies.

F.name: Ai NIKKOR 24mm f/2
F.note: Fast wide-angle with CRC floating element. Ai and Ai-S variants; the Ai-S is a common adapter cine lens.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 24mm f/2.8
F.note: Classic 24mm wide with CRC. Ai-S half-stop click aperture. Very long production run; adapter-friendly on mirrorless.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 28mm f/2
F.note: Fast 28mm with CRC. Reputed for smooth rendering wide open. Sought-after cine-mod candidate.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 28mm f/2.8
F.note: Highly regarded compact 28mm with CRC — many consider it optically the best manual 28mm Nikkor. 0.2m MFD.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 28mm f/3.5
F.note: Compact economy 28mm. Ai-S version continues the classic pre-Ai/K design.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 35mm f/1.4
F.note: Legendary fast 35mm — signature swirly wide-open rendering with prominent coma / spherochromatism giving a painterly look. Cult-classic for portrait and cinema.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 35mm f/2
F.note: Compact fast 35mm. Followed by AF-D 35mm f/2 in 1989.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 35mm f/2.8
F.note: Compact economy 35mm. Traditional retrofocus design.

F.name: Noct-NIKKOR 58mm f/1.2
F.note: Cult-classic hand-ground aspherical front element designed for point-light sources — near-zero coma wide open. The most collectible Ai/Ai-S Nikkor.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 50mm f/1.2
F.note: Fastest F-mount MF 50mm. Dreamy wide-open, sharpens up rapidly stopped down. Ai-S half-stop click.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 50mm f/1.4
F.note: Classic fast normal — the Ai-S version is the definitive MF Nikkor 50/1.4.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 50mm f/1.8 (Pancake)
F.note: Legendary short-barrel 'pancake' 50/1.8 — Japan-market series-E-influenced Ai-S. Extremely sharp; rare and collectible.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 50mm f/2
F.note: Traditional double-Gauss. Ai and Ai-S variants; entry-level normal replaced by 50/1.8 pancake.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 85mm f/1.4
F.note: 'Wilhelm' — the definitive MF portrait Nikkor. Legendary creamy bokeh and gentle wide-open rendering; cult classic for portrait photography.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 85mm f/2
F.note: Compact affordable portrait tele. Sonnar-derived design; well regarded on adapters.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 105mm f/1.8
F.note: Fast short tele; alternate to the classic 105/2.5. Only offered in Ai-S. Very sharp with beautiful subject separation.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 105mm f/2.5
F.note: Famous Sonnar-type — used by Steve McCurry for the 'Afghan Girl' portrait. One of the most celebrated Nikkors ever made for its rendering.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 135mm f/2
F.note: Fast long portrait tele. Built-in retractable hood. Superseded by AF DC 135/2.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 135mm f/2.8
F.note: Compact 135mm tele. Built-in retractable hood.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 135mm f/3.5
F.note: Compact economy 135mm. Long-lived design going back to the pre-Ai era.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 180mm f/2.8 ED
F.note: ED-glass fast tele. Highly regarded for sharpness even by modern standards; a favorite among adapter users.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 200mm f/4
F.note: Compact 200mm tele with built-in retractable hood.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 300mm f/2.8 ED
F.note: Fast super-tele with ED glass and internal focusing. Later revised as IF-ED variant.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 300mm f/4.5 ED (IF)
F.note: Compact ED IF super-tele. Popular field lens for wildlife/sports before AF era.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 400mm f/3.5 ED IF
F.note: Fast MF super-tele with ED glass and internal focus. 39mm rear drop-in filter.

F.name: Ai-S NIKKOR 500mm f/4P IF-ED
F.note: 'P' = CPU-chipped Ai-S — supports matrix metering on later F-mount bodies. ED glass, IF, 39mm rear drop-in.

F.name: Reflex-NIKKOR 500mm f/8
F.note: Catadioptric mirror lens (New/Ai-S). Famous 'donut' bokeh. Compact 500mm; fixed f/8, no aperture ring. Adapter cult favorite.

F.name: Reflex-NIKKOR 1000mm f/11
F.note: Catadioptric mirror super-tele. Compact given the focal length; classic 'donut' out-of-focus highlights.

F.name: Ai-S Micro-NIKKOR 55mm f/2.8
F.note: Classic 1:2 macro; 1:1 with PK-13 tube. CRC-corrected. Extremely sharp and long-lived design.

F.name: Ai-S Micro-NIKKOR 105mm f/2.8
F.note: 1:1 macro at native focus; CRC. Highly regarded for both macro and portraiture.

F.name: Ai-S Micro-NIKKOR 200mm f/4 IF
F.note: Long-working-distance 1:1 macro with IF. Precursor to AF-D Micro 200/4.

F.name: PC-NIKKOR 28mm f/3.5
F.note: Perspective-control (shift only) 28mm. +/-11mm shift. Manual preset aperture.

F.name: PC-NIKKOR 35mm f/2.8
F.note: Second-generation PC 35mm (multi-coated). +/-11mm shift. Manual preset aperture.

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 150-400mm F4.5 TC1.25x IS PRO

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 12-200mm F3.5-6.3

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 14-150mm F4.0-5.6 II
MicroFourThirds.note: 防滴処理は保護等級1級（IPX1）／防塵設計。JANはOM SYSTEM銘へ銘板変更された現行SKU（2022年11月25日発売）のもの。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 40-150mm F4.0-5.6 R
MicroFourThirds.note: ブラック代表（シルバー JAN 4545350054281）。JANはOM SYSTEM銘へ銘板変更された現行SKUのもの。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 75-300mm F4.8-6.7 II
MicroFourThirds.note: 最短撮影距離は75mm時0.9m、それ以外の焦点距離では1.5m。JANはOM SYSTEM銘へ銘板変更された現行SKUのもの。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 8mm F1.8 Fisheye PRO
MicroFourThirds.note: 防塵防滴 IP53相当。対角線画角180°の魚眼レンズで出目金前玉のためフィルター装着不可。JANはOM SYSTEM銘へ銘板変更された現行SKU（2024年6月21日発売）のもの（旧SKUは4545350047900）。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 17mm F1.2 PRO
MicroFourThirds.note: 防滴処理は保護等級1級（IPX1）／防塵設計。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 20mm F1.4 PRO
MicroFourThirds.note: 防滴性能は保護等級1級（IPX1）／防塵設計。OM Digital Solutions発売だが鏡筒銘板はOLYMPUS表記で、OM SYSTEM銘への切り替え前（2021年12月10日発売）の製品。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 25mm F1.2 PRO
MicroFourThirds.note: 防滴処理は保護等級1級（IPX1）／防塵設計。JANはOM SYSTEM銘へ銘板変更された現行SKU（2024年8月23日発売）のもの。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 45mm F1.2 PRO
MicroFourThirds.note: 防滴処理は保護等級1級（IPX1）／防塵設計。JANはOM SYSTEM銘へ銘板変更された現行SKUのもの。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 300mm F4.0 IS PRO
MicroFourThirds.note: 防塵防滴 IP53相当。全長はフード収納時（フード使用時280mm）。質量は三脚座を除く値（三脚座込み1,475g）。JANはOM SYSTEM銘へ銘板変更された現行SKU（2025年6月27日発売）のもの。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 12mm F2.0
MicroFourThirds.note: ブラック代表（シルバー JAN 4545350036416）。発売日は初出のシルバーのもので、ブラック単体発売は2014年9月20日。JANはOM SYSTEM銘へ銘板変更されたブラック現行SKU（2026年5月29日発売）のもの。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL 45mm F1.8
MicroFourThirds.note: ブラック代表（シルバー JAN 4545350037093）。発売日は初出のシルバーのもので、ブラック単体発売は2013年6月14日。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 75mm F1.8
MicroFourThirds.note: ブラック代表（シルバー JAN 4545350041496）。発売日は初出のシルバーのもの。JANはOM SYSTEM銘へ銘板変更されたブラック現行SKU（2024年9月20日発売）のもの。

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-Fisheye 8mm F/2.8
OM.note: Circular fisheye projecting a ~23mm image circle on 35mm film. Built-in turret with four filters (Y48/O56/R60/Sky). Multi-coated. Cult item on mirrorless for immersive 180-degree effect.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-Fisheye 16mm F/3.5
OM.note: Full-frame diagonal fisheye (180 deg diagonal). Built-in turret filters (Y48/O56/R60/Sky). Multi-coated. Remarkably compact for a fisheye — 180 g.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-W 18mm F/3.5
OM.note: Rare ultra-wide rectilinear. Multi-coated. Late addition to the OM line; low production numbers make this a collector piece.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-W 21mm F/2
OM.note: Fastest 21mm ever made for a 35mm SLR at launch. Multi-coated. Cult-classic on mirrorless — legendary rendering wide-open.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-W 21mm F/3.5
OM.note: Compact ultra-wide — pancake-like at 31 mm long. Multi-coated. Highly regarded for its size-to-quality ratio.

OM.name: Zuiko Shift 24mm F/3.5
OM.note: Shift lens (no tilt) with +/-10 mm rise/fall and 360-degree rotation. Multi-coated. Rare architectural specialty.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-W 24mm F/2
OM.note: Fast 24mm — one of the earliest f/2 24mm SLR lenses. Multi-coated. Prized on mirrorless for character and compactness.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-W 24mm F/2.8
OM.note: Compact wide standard. Later black-nose version is multi-coated; earliest silver-nose is single-coated. Classic OM travel companion.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-W 28mm F/2
OM.note: Fast wide with cult reputation for its 'creamy' rendering wide-open. Multi-coated. Popular on Sony A7 series.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-W 28mm F/2.8
OM.note: Compact everyday wide. Multi-coated. Replaced the earlier 28mm f/3.5 as the budget option in the OM catalogue.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-W 28mm F/3.5
OM.note: Original OM-1 launch wide. Later black-nose examples are multi-coated; earliest silver-nose are single-coated. Superseded by the 28mm f/2.8.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-W 35mm F/2
OM.note: Fast semi-wide. Multi-coated. Popular reportage focal length; excellent on mirrorless for street work.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-W 35mm F/2.8
OM.note: Original OM-1 launch semi-wide. Multi-coated in later production (earliest examples single-coated silver-nose).

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-Macro 20mm F/2 (bellows)
OM.note: Bellows-only high-magnification macro (approx 4x to 12x on Auto Bellows). No focus helicoid, no filter thread. Cult-classic ultra-macro optic.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-S 40mm F/2 (pancake)
OM.note: Cult-classic pancake — only 25 mm long. Multi-coated. Low production; commands high prices on the used market.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-S 50mm F/1.2
OM.note: Top-of-line OM normal. Multi-coated. Very fast for a compact SLR normal; popular for cinematic look on mirrorless.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-S 50mm F/1.4
OM.note: The definitive OM normal. Later black-nose examples with 'MC' marking are multi-coated; earliest silver-nose f/1.4 are single-coated. Late serials (~1.1M+) are widely reputed as improved.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-S 50mm F/1.8
OM.note: Kit normal. Later black-nose 'Made in Japan' version is multi-coated and often considered the best of the run. Extremely light at 170 g.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-Macro 50mm F/2
OM.note: Auto (helicoid) macro to 1:2 unaided, 1:1 with the dedicated Auto Extension Tube 25. Multi-coated. Highly regarded APO-like performance.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-Macro 50mm F/3.5
OM.note: Original OM macro. Auto (helicoid) to 1:2, 1:1 with Auto Extension Tube 25. Multi-coated in later production. Compact and beloved for its bite.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-Macro 90mm F/2
OM.note: Legendary short-tele macro — 1:2 unaided, 1:1 with Auto Extension Tube 25. Multi-coated with ED-like corrections. One of the finest MF macros ever made.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-T 85mm F/2
OM.note: Compact fast portrait. Multi-coated. Excellent Zuiko rendering — classic Sonnar-family design.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-T 100mm F/2
OM.note: Cult-classic fast portrait tele. Multi-coated with floating elements. Considered one of the finest 100mm f/2 lenses of the MF era; commands high used prices.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-T 100mm F/2.8
OM.note: Compact portrait tele. Multi-coated. Extremely light and pocketable for the focal length.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-T 135mm F/2.8
OM.note: Fast 135mm tele. Multi-coated. Built-in retractable hood.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-T 135mm F/3.5
OM.note: Compact 135mm. Multi-coated. Built-in retractable hood. Classic OM travel tele.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-Macro 135mm F/4.5 (bellows)
OM.note: Bellows-only macro (short mount, no focus helicoid). Also mounts in reverse on Auto Bellows for magnifications up to ~2x. Highly corrected flat-field optic.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-T 180mm F/2 ED
OM.note: Rare super-fast tele with ED glass. Multi-coated. Sports/portrait specialist; produced in small numbers, coveted collector item.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-T 180mm F/2.8
OM.note: Compact fast tele. Multi-coated. Built-in tripod collar and retractable hood.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-T 200mm F/4
OM.note: Compact 200mm. Multi-coated. Built-in retractable hood. A staple of OM tele kits.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-T 200mm F/5
OM.note: Budget compact 200mm — extraordinarily light. Multi-coated. Rare and sought-after for its size.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-T 300mm F/4.5
OM.note: Standard-fast 300mm tele. Multi-coated. Built-in tripod collar and retractable hood.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-T 350mm F/2.8 ED IF
OM.note: Flagship OM super-tele — ED glass, internal focus, rear filter drawer (46mm). Multi-coated. Extremely rare, a sports-photojournalism unicorn.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-T 400mm F/6.3
OM.note: Compact 400mm tele. Multi-coated. Built-in tripod collar. Uncommon on the used market.

OM.name: Zuiko Reflex 500mm F/8
OM.note: Catadioptric (mirror) super-tele. Multi-coated. Rear-mounted 30.5 mm filters (ND/YA2/O56/R60). Very compact for 500mm; produces characteristic ring-shaped bokeh.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-Zoom 35-70mm F/3.6
OM.note: Constant-aperture standard zoom. Multi-coated. Well-regarded optically; more solid than the later variable-aperture 35-70 replacements.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-Zoom 35-105mm F/3.5-4.5
OM.note: Wide-range standard zoom with pseudo-macro mode (0.4 m at wide). Multi-coated. Popular late-OM travel zoom.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-Zoom 65-200mm F/4
OM.note: Constant f/4 tele zoom. Multi-coated. Built-in tripod collar. Regarded as one of the better OM tele zooms.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-Zoom 75-150mm F/4
OM.note: Compact constant f/4 short tele zoom. Multi-coated. Extraordinarily light for the class.

OM.name: Zuiko Auto-Zoom 100-200mm F/5
OM.note: Early compact tele zoom. Multi-coated. Superseded by the 65-200 f/4 and 75-150 f/4.

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 12-40mm F2.8 PRO II
MicroFourThirds.note: 防塵防滴 IP53。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 40-150mm F4.0 PRO
MicroFourThirds.note: 防塵防滴 IP53。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 50-200mm F2.8 IS PRO
MicroFourThirds.note: 防塵防滴機構 IP53相当。質量は三脚座を除く値（三脚座込み1,250g）。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 9-18mm F4.0-5.6 II
MicroFourThirds.note: 公式仕様で防滴性能／防塵機構は「非対応」。最大撮影倍率はTele端の値（Wide端0.05倍）。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 150-600mm F5.0-6.3 IS
MicroFourThirds.note: 防滴性能は保護等級1級（IPX1）／防塵機構搭載。最短撮影距離・最大撮影倍率はWide端の値（Tele端2.8m／0.20倍）。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL 17mm F1.8 II
MicroFourThirds.note: 防滴性能は保護等級1級（IPX1）／防塵機構搭載。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL 25mm F1.8 II
MicroFourThirds.note: 防滴性能は保護等級1級（IPX1）／防塵機構搭載。JANは公式・価格.comとも未掲載のためID はスラッグ。

MicroFourThirds.name: M.ZUIKO DIGITAL ED 90mm F3.5 Macro IS PRO

L.name: LUMIX S 14-28mm F4-5.6 MACRO

M.name: Ksana 21mm f/3.5 ASPH.
M.note: Silver model TP-K21M-SR. Epoch Coating .73 for 1970s-style amber flare. Rangefinder coupling down to 0.7m, focuses to 0.5m via live view.

M.name: Simera 28mm f/1.4 ASPH.
M.note: Silver (TP-S28M-SR) and black (TP-S28M-BLK) finishes available as separate SKUs. Rangefinder-coupled with 6-bit coding. 14-blade circular aperture with de-click switch. 1 aspherical + 1 ED + 3 HRI elements with floating group. Japan release via STKB 2024-09-19; original global launch January 2024.

M.name: Simera 35mm f/1.4 ASPH.
M.note: Silver (TP-S35M-SR) and black (TP-S35M-BLK) finishes available as separate SKUs. Rangefinder-coupled with 6-bit coding. 14-blade circular aperture with de-click switch. 1 aspherical + 3 special elements. Features 1950s-style red-dot depth-of-field scale.

M.name: Simera 50mm f/1.4 ASPH.
M.note: Silver (TP-S50M-SR) and black (TP-S50M-BLK) finishes available as separate SKUs. Rangefinder-coupled with 6-bit coding. 1 aspherical + 1 ED + 3 HRI elements. Outstanding 0.45m minimum focus with 1:6.4 max magnification. Aperture range f/1.4-f/16.

M.name: Simera 75mm f/1.4 ASPH.
M.note: Silver (TP-S75M-SR) and black (TP-S75M-BLK) finishes available as separate SKUs. Rangefinder-coupled with 6-bit coding. 1 aspherical + 2 ED + 2 HRI elements. 16-blade diaphragm for perfectly round bokeh across f/1.4-f/16. Shares optical design with Simera-C 75mm T1.5 cinema lens. Aperture de-click switch on barrel.

M.name: Simera 28mm f/1.4 ASPH. Type II
M.note: Type II refresh with improved rangefinder-coupled distance meter accuracy and choice of infinity-lock or crescent-shaped focus tab. Silver and black finishes available as separate SKUs. Japan MSRP ¥124,000 tax-included at launch.

M.name: Simera 35mm f/1.4 ASPH. Type II
M.note: Type II refresh with improved rangefinder-coupled distance meter accuracy and choice of infinity-lock or crescent-shaped focus tab. Silver and black finishes available as separate SKUs. Japan MSRP ¥116,000 tax-included at launch.

E.name: Simera 28mm f/1.4 ASPH.
E.note: Sony E mirrorless-native version. TP-S28E-BLK (black). Silver may be a separate SKU. 14-blade aperture, aperture de-click switch, Visifocus depth scale.

E.name: Simera 35mm f/1.4 ASPH.
E.note: Sony E mirrorless-native version. Black and silver finishes available. 1 aspherical + 3 special elements, 14-blade aperture with de-click switch.

Z.name: Simera 28mm f/1.4 ASPH.
Z.note: Nikon Z mirrorless-native version. TP-S28Z-BLK (black) and TP-S28Z-SR (silver) available as separate SKUs.

Z.name: Simera 35mm f/1.4 ASPH.
Z.note: Nikon Z mirrorless-native version. Black and silver finishes available. 1 aspherical + 3 special elements, 14-blade aperture with de-click switch.

Z.name: Simera 50mm f/1.4 ASPH.
Z.note: Nikon Z mirrorless-native version. TP-S50Z-BLK (black) and TP-S50Z-SR (silver) available as separate SKUs. Japan MSRP ¥104,000 tax-included at launch.

RF.name: Simera 28mm f/1.4 ASPH.
RF.note: Canon RF mirrorless-native version. TP-S28RF-BLK (black) and TP-S28RF-SRR (silver) available as separate SKUs.

CY.name: Distagon T* 15mm F3.5
CY.note: Ultra-wide rectilinear. AEG (West Germany) production. Built-in filter turret (orange/yellow/UV/neutral). Legendary for architecture and landscape.

CY.name: Distagon T* 18mm F4
CY.note: AEG (West Germany) production. Very-wide reportage lens.

CY.name: Distagon T* 21mm F2.8
CY.note: MMG (Germany, multi-mode). Legendary status - one of the finest 21mm designs ever made. Extreme corner sharpness and low distortion.

CY.name: Distagon T* 25mm F2.8
CY.note: Available in AEG (West Germany) and MMJ (Japan) versions.

CY.name: Distagon T* 28mm F2 (Hollywood)
CY.note: Nicknamed 'Hollywood'. AEG and MMJ versions. Fast wide with cinematic rendering, popular for video adaptation.

CY.name: Distagon T* 28mm F2.8
CY.note: Compact wide. AEG (West Germany) and MMJ (Japan) versions.

CY.name: Distagon T* 35mm F1.4
CY.note: Cult-classic bokeh king. AEG (West Germany) most prized; MMJ also produced. Signature swirly out-of-focus rendering.

CY.name: Distagon T* 35mm F2.8
CY.note: Compact walk-around wide. AEG and MMJ versions.

CY.name: PC-Distagon T* 35mm F2.8
CY.note: Perspective-control shift lens (±10mm). AEG (West Germany) production. Rare architectural specialty.

CY.name: Planar T* 50mm F1.4
CY.note: Fast standard prime. AEG (West Germany) and MMJ (Japan) versions. Reference-quality double-Gauss.

CY.name: Planar T* 50mm F1.7
CY.note: Budget-friendly kit standard. AEG and MMJ versions. Often exceeds the 1.4 stopped down.

CY.name: Planar T* 55mm F1.2 (60 Jahre)
CY.note: Cult limited edition (~1000 units) marking Contax 60th anniversary. AEG (West Germany). Ultra-rare collector's item.

CY.name: Planar T* 85mm F1.2 (60 Jahre / 100 Jahre)
CY.note: Limited edition anniversary lens (60 Jahre 1982; 100 Jahre re-release 1996). AEG. Extremely rare and highly collectible.

CY.name: Planar T* 85mm F1.4
CY.note: Legendary portrait lens. AEG (West Germany) and MMJ (Japan) versions. Signature Zeiss 3D pop and creamy bokeh.

CY.name: Sonnar T* 85mm F2.8
CY.note: Compact classic Sonnar formula portrait. AEG and MMJ versions.

CY.name: Sonnar T* 100mm F3.5
CY.note: Slim short-tele. AEG and MMJ versions.

CY.name: Sonnar T* 135mm F2.8
CY.note: Classic Sonnar tele. AEG (West Germany) and MMJ (Japan) versions.

CY.name: Sonnar T* 180mm F2.8
CY.note: 'Olympic Sonnar' - held in high regard for sports and portrait. AEG (West Germany).

CY.name: Sonnar T* 200mm F4
CY.note: Compact medium-tele. AEG and MMJ versions.

CY.name: Sonnar T* 300mm F2.8
CY.note: Pro-grade fast tele. AEG (West Germany). Rear drop-in filter (39mm). Rare and prized.

CY.name: Tele-Tessar T* 300mm F4
CY.note: AEG (West Germany). Compact-for-the-class 300mm.

CY.name: Tele-Tessar T* 200mm F3.5
CY.note: AEG (West Germany). Rare tele-Tessar formula.

CY.name: Mirotar 500mm F8
CY.note: Mirror (catadioptric) super-tele. AEG (West Germany). Extremely rare - no T* multi-coating (mirror design).

CY.name: Mirotar 1000mm F5.6
CY.note: Legendary mirror super-tele. AEG (West Germany). One of the rarest Contax C/Y lenses ever produced - fewer than 20 units estimated.

CY.name: Makro-Planar T* 60mm F2.8 C
CY.note: 1:2 (0.5x) native, 1:1 with C/Y macro extension tube. AEG and MMJ versions. Superb flat-field rendering.

CY.name: Makro-Planar T* 100mm F2.8
CY.note: 1:2 native, 1:1 with extension. AEG (West Germany) production. Renowned as one of the finest macro lenses ever made.

CY.name: S-Planar T* 60mm F2.8
CY.note: Original macro-normal, later replaced by Makro-Planar 60/2.8 C. AEG (West Germany). 1:2 native reproduction.

CY.name: Vario-Sonnar T* 28-70mm F3.5-4.5
CY.note: MMJ (Japan). Compact standard zoom.

CY.name: Vario-Sonnar T* 35-70mm F3.4
CY.note: Constant F3.4 across the range. AEG (West Germany) and MMJ. One of the earliest premium standard zooms.

CY.name: Vario-Sonnar T* 80-200mm F4
CY.note: Constant F4 tele zoom. AEG (West Germany) and MMJ (Japan) versions. Reference-class tele zoom.

CY.name: Vario-Sonnar T* 100-300mm F4.5-5.6
CY.note: MMJ (Japan) production. Long tele zoom for the C/Y system.

CY.name: Hologon T* 16mm F8
CY.note: Ultra-wide rectilinear Hologon design (not fisheye). Fixed F8, no diaphragm. AEG (West Germany). Extremely rare - reissue of the classic Zeiss Hologon.

M42.name: Carl Zeiss Jena Flektogon 35mm f/2.8
